hero.subtitle:
With AI, you can fly.
The launchpad for the AI Agent era.
hero.funnel: Start free with Ollama → Deploy to Zeabur cloud → Expand AI skills

vision.descLeft1: The AI Agent market is exploding — from personal assistants to enterprise automation, 2026 is the year everyone can have an AI partner. But most people don't know where to start.
vision.descLeft2: What hardware to choose? How to install software? How to run an Agent? Canfly solves all these questions at once.
vision.descRight1: We are the knowledge guide to the AI Agent world. From free experiences to hardware recommendations, from white-glove setup to community support — we take you from zero to takeoff.
vision.descRight2: Not just a tool platform, but a community. Every user has their own flight log, showcasing your AI Agent, sharing your setup, and inspiring others.
vision.stat2Label: Want to use AI but don't know where to start
vision.stat3Label: From zero to takeoff with Canfly

ollama.title: Run Your First Local AI in 5 Minutes
ollama.subtitle: Free, offline, completely private — run large language models on your own computer with Ollama

steps.title: Choose Install Method
steps.content: Choose the installation method that works best for you. Mac beginners should download Ollama.app (includes a built-in chat interface); advanced users can use the terminal.
steps.expectedResult: After installation, Ollama will automatically start running in the background.
tips: Requires at least 8GB RAM (16GB+ recommended)
macOS 11.0 or higher required
No reboot needed after installation

items.a: Go to System Settings → Privacy & Security, click "Open Anyway". This is macOS's security mechanism for unsigned apps.
Reopen your terminal, or run source ~/.zshrc to reload environment variables.

items.q: npm permission error installing OpenClaw on Mac
items.a: Classic macOS issue with pkg-installed Node.js. Quick fix: sudo chown -R $(whoami) /usr/local/lib/node_modules/ /usr/local/bin/ — or better, use nvm to manage Node.js. Full guide: blog.juchunko.com/zh/ollama-openclaw-npm-permission-fix
Run the installer as Administrator. Right-click → Run as administrator.

installTabs.guiDesc:
Official Ollama macOS app with built-in graphical chat interface.
Download, drag to Applications, double-click and start chatting with AI. No terminal, no commands needed.
guiSteps: Drag Ollama.app to the Applications folder
Double-click to open; the Ollama icon will appear in the menu bar
Click the icon, select "Chat" to open the built-in chat interface
Choose a model and start chatting!
installTabs.terminalConfirm: After installation, Ollama service will start automatically. Verify installation:
installTabs.winDesc: Download the Windows version from the official Ollama website and follow the installation wizard.

steps.title: Download Your First AI Model
steps.content: An AI model is like the AI's "brain". Different models are good at different things. Let's download a lightweight model suitable for chatting.

subsections.label: What is a model?
subsections.text: A model is an AI program trained on massive amounts of data. Like different experts having different specialties, different models excel at different tasks. Model file sizes range from 2GB to 40GB+.

steps.expectedResult: After download completes, it will show success. You can use ollama list to check installed models.
tips: First download requires internet; after that you can use completely offline
Model files are stored locally, no data is uploaded
Low on disk space? Just install llama3.2 for now

items.a: Model files are large, ensure stable internet. You can also try a smaller model like llama3.2.
Use ollama list to check installed models, use ollama rm <model> to remove unneeded models.

steps.title: Test Interactive Chat
steps.content: Let's chat with your local AI and make sure everything is working!
steps.expectedResult: The AI will respond to your questions with text. Response speed depends on your hardware specs.
tips: First time running a model is slower (needs to load into memory); it gets faster after
Press Ctrl+D or type /bye to exit the chat
Try asking questions in different languages to experience multilingual capabilities

items.a: Normal! Loading a model for the first time takes time. If consistently slow, try the smaller llama3.2 model.
Close other applications to free up memory, or switch to a smaller model.

steps.title: Connect to OpenClaw
steps.content: OpenClaw is an open-source AI interface that connects to your local Ollama, giving you a better user experience.
steps.expectedResult: OpenClaw can now use your local Ollama models. Completely free, completely offline!
tips: OpenClaw automatically detects all your installed models
You can switch between different models in OpenClaw at any time
Want to use it on your phone? You can later deploy to Zeabur cloud

items.a: Confirm Ollama is running: type ollama list in terminal. If no response, restart Ollama.

items.q: What to put for API address?
items.a: Default is http://localhost:11434, unless you've changed it.

items.q: Mac sleeps and AI agent goes offline?
items.a: Run: sudo pmset -c sleep 0 disksleep 0 networkoversleep 1 — then nohup caffeinate -s &. This keeps your Mac awake for 24/7 AI agent use. Full guide: blog.juchunko.com/zh/openclaw-always-on-macos

steps.title: Next Steps
steps.content: Congratulations! You've got AI running on your own computer. Here's what to explore next:
tips: All models are free to use
Your data never leaves your computer
Join the Canfly community to connect with other users

nextStepCards.title: Deploy to Cloud
nextStepCards.desc: One-click deploy with Zeabur to make your AI available anywhere. Use code OpenClaw for 10% off.

nextStepCards.title: Add Voice Features
nextStepCards.desc: Use ElevenLabs to give your AI a voice, with Chinese text-to-speech support.

nextStepCards.desc: Give your AI agent Web3 superpowers — wallets, on-chain identity, email, and more. Works with 40+ AI coding agents.

nextStepCards.title: Explore More Models
nextStepCards.desc: Browse hundreds of models in the Ollama library: text, code, vision, and more.

zeabur.title: Deploy OpenClaw to Zeabur Cloud in 5 Minutes
zeabur.subtitle: Take your local AI global — one-click deployment with custom domain, HTTPS, and 24/7 availability

steps.title: Create Zeabur Account
steps.content: Sign up for Zeabur using our affiliate link to get 10% off your first deployment. No credit card required to start.
steps.expectedResult: You'll have access to the Zeabur dashboard and can start deploying immediately.
tips: Use code 'OpenClaw' at checkout for 10% discount
Free tier includes 3 projects with generous resource limits
GitHub integration makes deployment seamless

items.a: Check spam folder. If still missing, try signing up with a different email provider like Gmail.
Make sure you allow Zeabur access to your repositories during the OAuth flow.

steps.title: Fork OpenClaw Repository
steps.content: Create your own copy of the OpenClaw repository on GitHub so you can customize and deploy it.
steps.expectedResult: You'll have a fork of OpenClaw in your GitHub account ready for deployment.
tips: Keep the repository name as 'openclaw' for easier identification
You can make it private if you prefer
The fork will sync with upstream OpenClaw updates

items.a: Create one at github.com — it's free and takes 2 minutes. You'll need it for most cloud deployments.
Make sure you're logged into GitHub and viewing the official OpenClaw repository.

steps.title: Deploy to Zeabur
steps.content: Connect your GitHub repository to Zeabur and deploy with one click. Zeabur will automatically detect it's a Node.js app and configure everything.
steps.expectedResult: Your OpenClaw instance will be live with a temporary zeabur.app domain and automatic HTTPS.
tips: Zeabur auto-detects the build configuration
Deployment typically takes 1-2 minutes
You get a free .zeabur.app subdomain immediately

items.a: Check the build logs in Zeabur dashboard. Most issues are missing environment variables or incorrect Node.js version.
Ensure you've connected your GitHub account and the repository is accessible to Zeabur.

steps.title: Configure Environment Variables
steps.content: Set up your OpenAI API key and other configuration variables to make your cloud instance functional.
steps.expectedResult: OpenClaw will be able to connect to AI models and function properly in the cloud.
tips: Store sensitive keys in Zeabur's environment variables (encrypted)
You can use OpenAI, Anthropic Claude, or other supported models
Set OLLAMA_HOST if you want to connect back to your local Ollama

items.q: Where to get OpenAI API key
items.a: Visit platform.openai.com, create an account, and generate an API key. You'll need to add billing info.

items.q: App not restarting after env changes
items.a: In Zeabur dashboard, manually restart your service after updating environment variables.

steps.title: Add Custom Domain (Optional)
steps.content: Connect your own domain name to make your AI assistant accessible at your preferred URL.
steps.expectedResult: Your OpenClaw instance will be available at your custom domain with automatic SSL.
tips: You can use any domain you own
Zeabur provides free SSL certificates automatically
DNS changes may take up to 24 hours to propagate

items.a: Check that your DNS CNAME record points to your zeabur.app domain. Use dig or nslookup to verify.
Wait 5-10 minutes after DNS propagation. Zeabur automatically issues Let's Encrypt certificates.

steps.title: Test Your Cloud AI
steps.content: Congratulations! Your AI assistant is now available 24/7 from anywhere in the world. Test it out and share the link.
steps.expectedResult: You can access your AI from any device, anywhere, and share it with others.
tips: Bookmark your new URL for easy access
Share with team members or friends to try
Monitor usage in Zeabur dashboard to track costs

nextStepCards.title: Add Voice with ElevenLabs
nextStepCards.desc: Give your cloud AI a voice with ElevenLabs integration. Support for multiple languages and natural voices.

nextStepCards.title: Create AI Videos
nextStepCards.desc: Use HeyGen to create AI avatar videos that showcase your deployed agent.

nextStepCards.desc: Advanced: Configure Headless Chrome for your cloud AI agent. Troubleshooting guide for browser integration.

nextStepCards.title: Join the Community
nextStepCards.desc: Share your deployment in the Canfly community and inspire others to take their AI to the cloud.

elevenlabs.title: Add Voice to OpenClaw with ElevenLabs in 5 Minutes
elevenlabs.subtitle: Transform text into natural speech — give your AI agents realistic voices with ElevenLabs integration

steps.title: Create ElevenLabs Account
steps.content: Sign up for ElevenLabs using our affiliate link to get started with AI voice synthesis. Free tier includes 10,000 characters per month.
steps.expectedResult: You'll have access to ElevenLabs dashboard with API key for integration.
tips: Free tier: 10,000 characters/month (about 10-15 minutes of audio)
Starter plan: 30,000 characters for $5/month with our 22% affiliate program
Over 29 languages supported including English and Chinese

items.a: Check spam folder. ElevenLabs sometimes takes a few minutes to send confirmation emails.
Go to Profile Settings → API Key. You may need to verify your email first.

steps.title: Get Your API Key
steps.content: Copy your ElevenLabs API key from the dashboard. You'll need this to connect ElevenLabs to OpenClaw.
steps.expectedResult: You have your ElevenLabs API key ready for integration.
tips: Keep your API key private — never share it publicly
You can regenerate the key if it gets compromised
Free tier has rate limits, paid plans have higher limits

items.a: Make sure you copied the entire key without extra spaces. Some browsers add spaces when copying.
Your API key may be invalid or your account needs email verification.

steps.title: Configure OpenClaw Integration
steps.content: Add the ElevenLabs skill to your OpenClaw setup and configure it with your API key.
steps.expectedResult: OpenClaw can now generate speech from text using ElevenLabs voices.
tips: Test with a short sentence first to verify the integration works
You can change voices in the ElevenLabs dashboard
Monitor your character usage to avoid hitting limits

items.a: Make sure you have the latest version of OpenClaw and restart the application.
Check your system audio settings. Some browsers block auto-play audio by default.

steps.title: Live Voice Demo
steps.content: Test the integration with a real conversation. Ask your OpenClaw agent to speak its response using ElevenLabs voices.
steps.expectedResult: You hear natural AI speech generated from your OpenClaw agent's text responses.
tips: Try different languages to test multilingual capability
Experiment with voice settings in ElevenLabs dashboard
Longer responses take more characters from your quota

items.a: Try a different voice model in ElevenLabs. Premium voices sound more natural than free tier voices.
Check your internet connection. Audio streaming requires stable bandwidth.

steps.title: Voice Use Cases & Next Steps
steps.content: Congratulations! Your AI can now speak. Explore advanced voice features and practical applications.
tips: Perfect for accessibility — visually impaired users can hear responses
Great for content creation — convert articles to podcasts
Educational use — language learning with native pronunciation

nextStepCards.title: Upgrade to Starter Plan
nextStepCards.desc: Get 30,000 characters, premium voices, and commercial usage. Support Canfly with 22% affiliate commission.

nextStepCards.title: Custom Voice Cloning
nextStepCards.desc: Clone your own voice or create brand-specific voices with ElevenLabs Voice Lab.

nextStepCards.desc: Combine ElevenLabs voices with HeyGen AI avatars to create complete video content.

nextStepCards.title: Deploy Voice to Cloud
nextStepCards.desc: Use Zeabur to deploy your voice-enabled AI agent globally with 24/7 availability.

virtualmachine.title: Zero-to-Hero: Run AI in a Virtual Machine in 10 Minutes
virtualmachine.subtitle: No tech experience needed — learn what a VM is, install Linux with one click, and run your own AI inside it

steps.title: What Is a Virtual Machine?
steps.content: A virtual machine (VM) is like a computer inside your computer. It lets you run a completely separate operating system — like Linux — inside a window on your Mac, without affecting anything on your main system. Think of it as a sandbox: you can experiment freely, and if anything goes wrong, just delete the VM and start over.

subsections.label: Why use a VM for AI?
subsections.text: Running AI models in a VM keeps your main system clean. You get a dedicated Linux environment optimized for AI tools like Ollama, without worrying about messing up your Mac's settings. It's also great for learning Linux basics in a safe environment.

subsections.label: UTM vs Virtual Buddy
subsections.text: Both are Mac apps for running VMs. Virtual Buddy is simpler — one-click Linux install, perfect for beginners. UTM is free and more powerful, with support for many operating systems. We'll cover both so you can choose.

steps.expectedResult: You understand what a VM is and why it's useful for running AI. You're ready to pick your tool.
tips: VMs are completely isolated — nothing you do inside affects your Mac
You can run multiple VMs at once if you have enough RAM
Apple Silicon Macs (M1/M2/M3/M4) run Linux VMs extremely fast

items.q: Will a VM slow down my Mac?
items.a: Only slightly while the VM is running. Close the VM and your Mac returns to full speed. With 16GB+ RAM, you won't notice any slowdown.

items.q: Is this the same as Docker?
items.a: Similar idea, but VMs run a full operating system with its own desktop. Docker runs lightweight containers without a full OS. For beginners, VMs are easier to understand.

steps.title: Install UTM or Virtual Buddy
steps.content: Choose one: Virtual Buddy for the easiest experience (paid, from Mac App Store), or UTM for a free, full-featured option. Both work great on Apple Silicon Macs.
steps.expectedResult: You have UTM or Virtual Buddy installed and ready to create your first VM.
tips: Virtual Buddy: $10 on Mac App Store, one-click Linux setup — perfect for beginners
UTM: 100% free and open source, more configuration options
Both support Apple Silicon natively for best performance

items.a: Go to System Settings → Privacy & Security, click "Open Anyway". This is macOS's standard security for apps downloaded outside the App Store.
Virtual Buddy needs macOS Ventura or later. If you're on an older version, use UTM instead — it supports macOS 11+.
If you want zero hassle and don't mind paying $10, go with Virtual Buddy. If you prefer free software or want more control, choose UTM.

steps.title: Create a Linux VM
steps.content: Now let's create your Linux virtual machine. Virtual Buddy makes this a one-click process. With UTM, you'll download an Ubuntu image and follow a short setup wizard.

subsections.label: Why Ubuntu?
subsections.text: Ubuntu is the most beginner-friendly Linux distribution. It has a graphical desktop, tons of online help, and excellent support for AI tools. Most AI tutorials and documentation assume Ubuntu.

steps.expectedResult: A Linux (Ubuntu) VM is running in a window on your Mac. You can see the Ubuntu desktop or terminal.
tips: Allocate at least 4GB RAM and 2 CPU cores to the VM for smooth AI performance
20GB disk space is enough to start; you can expand later
The VM downloads Ubuntu automatically — no need to find ISO files yourself with Virtual Buddy

items.a: Make sure you allocated enough RAM (4GB minimum). Close other heavy apps on your Mac. Also ensure you're using the ARM64 version of Ubuntu, not x86.

items.q: Screen is black after creating VM
items.a: Wait 1-2 minutes — the first boot takes time to initialize. If still black, try restarting the VM.

items.q: Where to download Ubuntu ISO for UTM?
items.a: Go to ubuntu.com/download/server and download the ARM64 version for Apple Silicon Macs.

steps.title: Install Ollama in Your VM
steps.content: Open the terminal inside your Linux VM and install Ollama with one command. Then download an AI model and start chatting — all running inside your virtual machine!
steps.expectedResult: Ollama is installed in your VM and you can chat with a local AI model. Everything runs inside the VM, completely isolated.
tips: The curl command downloads and installs Ollama automatically
First model download (llama3.2) is about 2GB — make sure your VM has internet
Type /bye to exit the chat when you're done

items.a: Run: sudo apt install curl — then retry the Ollama install command.
In UTM, go to VM Settings → Network and ensure "Shared" networking is selected. In Virtual Buddy, networking should work automatically.
VM network can be slower than host. Be patient, or try the smaller llama3.2 model (2GB) first.
Increase the VM's RAM allocation to at least 4GB. Shut down the VM first, adjust settings, then restart.

steps.title: Next Steps
steps.content: Amazing! You've built your own AI lab inside a virtual machine. Your Mac stays clean, and you have a full Linux environment for AI experiments. Here's where to go next:
tips: Your VM is saved — close it and reopen anytime without losing your setup
You can snapshot your VM to save its current state as a backup
Everything you learned here applies to real Linux servers too

heygen.title: Create AI Avatar Videos with HeyGen + OpenClaw in 5 Minutes
heygen.subtitle: Transform your AI conversations into professional avatar videos — perfect for content creation and training

steps.title: Create HeyGen Account
steps.content: Sign up for HeyGen using our affiliate link. Free tier includes 1 credit (1-minute video) to test the platform.
steps.expectedResult: You'll have access to HeyGen Studio with API access for integration.
tips: Free tier: 1 credit (1-minute video) for testing
Creator plan: $29/month with our 20% affiliate commission
100+ avatars, 300+ voices, 40+ languages available

items.a: Free credits may take a few minutes to appear. Refresh the page or contact HeyGen support.
API access requires a paid plan. Free tier is for manual studio use only.

steps.title: Get API Access
steps.content: Generate your HeyGen API key from the developer dashboard to enable programmatic video creation.
steps.expectedResult: You have API credentials ready for OpenClaw integration.
tips: API access requires Creator plan ($29/month) or higher
Keep your API key secure — it controls video generation credits
Each API call consumes credits based on video length

items.a: Upgrade to Creator plan first. API access is not available on free tier.
Verify your plan has remaining credits. Check the developer console for error messages.

steps.title: Configure OpenClaw + HeyGen
steps.content: Set up the HeyGen integration in OpenClaw to automatically convert AI responses into avatar videos.
steps.expectedResult: OpenClaw can generate avatar videos from text responses using HeyGen API.
tips: Start with a simple avatar to test the integration
Video generation takes 30-60 seconds per minute of content
You can customize avatar, voice, and background

items.a: Normal for first video. HeyGen processes videos in queue during peak times.
Make sure you selected the correct avatar ID. Preview in HeyGen Studio first.

steps.title: Create Your First AI Video
steps.content: Generate your first AI avatar video by asking your OpenClaw agent a question and having it respond with video.
steps.expectedResult: You have a professional AI avatar video generated from your conversation.
tips: Keep initial videos short (30-60 seconds) to test
Try different avatars to find your preferred style
Videos are perfect for social media, education, and presentations

items.a: Check your internet connection during download. Re-generate if needed.

items.q: Audio doesn't sync with avatar lips
items.a: This can happen with very long sentences. Try shorter, more natural speech patterns.

steps.title: Video Use Cases & Advanced Features
steps.content: Congratulations! You can now create AI avatar videos. Explore advanced features and business applications.
tips: Perfect for educational content — explain complex topics with avatars
Social media content — create engaging posts automatically
Customer support — personalize responses with video messages

hardwareCompare.title: AI Hardware Buying Guide
hardwareCompare.subtitle: Umbrel Home vs Mac mini vs Raspberry Pi — which one is right for you? Compare price, difficulty, and best use cases.

devices.umbrel-homeDesc: Purpose-built home server with one-click app store. Plug in, set up in 10 minutes, and run AI + self-hosted apps 24/7 with zero maintenance.
devices.mac-miniDesc: Apple's compact powerhouse. M-series chips deliver excellent AI inference performance. Best for developers who also need a desktop workstation.
devices.raspberry-piDesc: The classic DIY single-board computer. Ultra-affordable entry point for tinkerers, learners, and lightweight AI experiments.

rec.umbrelDesc: You want a plug-and-play home server that runs AI models, Bitcoin nodes, and self-hosted apps without touching a terminal. Perfect for non-technical users who value privacy and sovereignty.
rec.macDesc: You need a powerful development machine that doubles as an AI inference server. M-series chips run Ollama models fast, and you get a full macOS desktop for coding.
rec.piDesc: You want the cheapest possible entry point and enjoy building things from scratch. Great for learning, lightweight AI tasks, and IoT projects. Runs small models only.

hardwareCompare.ctaText: No matter which hardware you pick, start with our free Ollama tutorial to get your first AI running in 5 minutes.

vm.whySubtitle: A safe playground for your AI journey
vm.whyDesc: A virtual machine (VM) is like a computer inside your computer. It creates a completely isolated environment where you can install and experiment with AI tools — without any risk to your main system.
vm.benefit1Desc: Install anything, break anything. Your real computer stays untouched. If something goes wrong, just delete the VM and start fresh.
vm.benefit2Desc: Not sure if AI is right for you? Try it in a VM first. No commitment, no risk, completely free.
vm.benefit3Desc: Once you're comfortable, you can install directly on your computer for better performance — or keep using VMs for isolation.
vm.pathBeginnerDesc: Start with Virtual Buddy — one click and you have Linux ready for AI.
vm.pathIntermediate: I know a bit about computers
vm.pathIntermediateDesc: Use UTM for more control. Create VMs for different purposes.
vm.pathDirect: I just want to install directly
vm.pathDirectDesc: Skip VMs entirely. Install Ollama straight on your Mac, Linux, or Windows.
